goal: Fix the real operations-center failure and refine the UI shell so second-level navigation lives in the left sidebar.
deliverable: Header cleanup that preserves title and scoped search
Governed verification against the real current database plus baseline smoke
constraints: Stay on the current stack and current hash-based navigation model
Avoid turning the entire shell into a deep tree
Preserve existing reports and operations subviews
acceptance_criteria: The reproduced operations-center snapshot path no longer raises on the current database
Second-level entries move into the left sidebar for subview-enabled areas
Header no longer duplicates the submenu row
Baseline validation and touched-file syntax checks pass
product_acceptance_criteria: Operators can switch reports and operations subviews from the shell without returning to the page header
Operations center remains usable even when recommendation data is incomplete
manual_spot_checks: Open operations and switch across overview, procurement, master data, and exceptions from the sidebar
Open reports and switch to tracker and suppliers from the sidebar
Confirm the search input still appears only in subviews where narrowing visible rows helps
Load the operations-center snapshot against the current repo database
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after reproducing and fixing the real crash path, then rerunning validation.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the sidebar submenu move, the operations-center hardening, and the verification that actually ran.
non_goals: Global navigation redesign for every product area
A complete component extraction of the workbench
inferred_assumptions: The user wants the sidebar to become the main place for contextual second-level navigation.
The reported 500 is more urgent than any further UI polish and must be fixed in the same round.
